preview_text:
The model backs Spain as a clear favorite with a 71% win probability, leaving Uruguay with only an 8% chance of victory. This represents a substantial gap in the forecast; Spain enters as the heavily preferred side on the basis of the underlying Elo ratings supplied to the Dixon-Coles calculation.

The expected match shape leans toward a Spain win, but the 22% draw probability indicates the result is not entirely one-sided. Uruguay's 8% win chance reflects material underdog status, yet a draw remains a plausible outcome—roughly one in five runs of this fixture. The spread across the three outcomes suggests competitive play rather than a predetermined outcome, even with Spain favored.

The sportsbook consensus materially diverges from the model. Books assign Uruguay nearly double the win probability (16% vs 8%), while pricing Spain's win at 61% compared to the model's 71%. The gap on the favorite amounts to roughly 10 percentage points. Polymarket prices align more closely with the model on Spain but remain materially higher on Uruguay. The dashboard does not adjudicate between these sources; the discrepancy warrants attention from users interpreting the forecast.
